description: 'Believe in something. Even if it means sacrificing everything.' Initial boycott then strong recovery and sales surge. Tests polarization-with-recovery dynamics where vocal minority is commercially irrelevant.

key_events.description: Kaepernick unveiled as face of 30th anniversary Just Do It campaign. Immediate polarization.
#BoycottNike peaks. Shoe burning videos. Trump tweets criticism. NKE stock drops 3.2%. BUT online sales up 31%.
Full 'Dream Crazy' TV ad airs during NFL opener. Widely praised. Sentiment shifts as broader audience engages.
Nike Q1 earnings beat: revenue up 10% to $9.94B. Financial proof campaign was a success.
NKE stock hits all-time high ~$83/share. ~$6B market value added since campaign.

calibration_notes: Check-mark shaped recovery. Initial dip driven by vocal minority amplified by social media and political figures (Trump). Nike's core customer base (younger, urban, diverse) was always supportive. Boycotters skewed older and were not Nike's target. Morning Consult: favorability dropped among Republicans but rose among Democrats and independents. Net positive among 18-34 from day one. Online sales surge 31% (Edison Trends). Revenue and stock data from Nike SEC filings.
